name: Pico Torrecillas – circular via the Cañada de las Ánimas - Sierra de las Nieves National Park
name_original: Pico Torrecillas – circular por la Cañada de las Ánimas - Parque Nacional de la Sierra de las Nieves
region: National Park Sierra de las Nieves
region_intro: Hiking around National Park Sierra de las Nieves offers diverse terrain, characterized by dramatic karst landscapes, high peaks, and unique Spanish fir forests. This UNESCO Biosphere Reserve features rugged beauty with impressive sinkholes and a complex network of geological formations. The park's varied elevation, including Torrecilla Peak at 1,919 meters, provides a range of challenges and panoramic views. Hikers can explore ancient woodlands, discover rare flora, and encounter diverse wildlife within this protected natural area. Best hiking trails around National Park Sierra de las Nieves The most popular hiking route is Parque Nacional de la Sierra de las Nieves – loop by the Cascadas Trail (PR-A 282), a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route features stunning waterfalls and lush natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Torrecillas – circular via the Cañada de las Ánimas - Sierra de las Nieves National Park, a difficult 9.3 miles (14.9 km) path. This trail offers significant elevation gains and rewarding panoramic views from the park's highest point. Local hikers also love the Parque Nacional de la Sierra de las Nieves – circular to Peñón de los Enamorados, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through dramatic karst landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 48 minutes. Hiking around National Park Sierra de las Nieves is defined by dramatic karst formations, ancient Spanish fir forests, and high mountain peaks. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely walks to challenging mountaineering. The routes in National Park Sierra de las Nieves are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,300 reviews. More than 8,000 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de las Nieves's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
A: Sierra de las Nieves National Park offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 45 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain expeditions, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in the park?
A: Yes, the park features several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. For instance, the Charco de la Virgen loop is known for leading to beautiful natural pools. You can also find 9 easy routes on komoot, perfect for a leisurely walk.

Q: What kind of unique natural features can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The park is renowned for its dramatic karst landscapes, featuring impressive sinkholes and high cliffs. A defining feature is its ancient Spanish fir (Abies pinsapo) forests, a rare relic from the Ice Age. You can also discover stunning waterfalls like La Rejía and Los Horcajuelos, and even historic 'neveros' or snow wells along some trails.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Absolutely. The park is home to several beautiful waterfalls. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995677601/parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-las-nieves-loop-by-the-cascadas-trail-pr-a-282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de la Sierra de las Nieves – loop by the Cascadas Trail (PR-A 282)&lt;/a&gt;, which features stunning waterfalls and lush natural surroundings. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3847454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del Canalón Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/719931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco de la Virgen Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; as highlights within the park.

Q: What is the highest point in the park, and are there trails to it?
A: The highest point in Sierra de las Nieves National Park is Torrecilla Peak, reaching 1,919 meters. For experienced hikers, the ascent to Torrecilla Peak offers challenging expeditions with significant elevation gains and rewarding panoramic views. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995685820/pico-torrecillas-circular-via-the-canada-de-las-animas-sierra-de-las-nieves-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Torrecillas – circular via the Cañada de las Ánimas&lt;/a&gt; for a challenging experience to the summit.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
A: Yes, there are many circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995687660/parque-nacional-de-la-sierra-de-las-nieves-circular-to-penon-de-los-enamorados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de la Sierra de las Nieves – circular to Peñón de los Enamorados&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option leading through dramatic karst landscapes. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10297643" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada de las Ánimas loop from Cortijo de los Quejigales&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I encounter while hiking in the park?
A: Sierra de las Nieves is rich in biodiversity. Hikers might spot Iberian ibex, golden eagles, peregrine falcons, and eagle owls. The park is also home to otters and wildcats, and its location on the migratory route of the Strait further enhances its animal diversity.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
A: The trails in Sierra de las Nieves National Park are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 1,300 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic karst formations, the unique Spanish fir forests, and the rewarding panoramic views from the high mountain peaks.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in the park?
A: While most trails are freely accessible, certain areas or activities within a National Park might require specific permits, especially for larger groups or protected zones. It's always advisable to check the official park website or local information centers for the most current regulations before your visit.

Q: What are the best starting points for hikes in Sierra de las Nieves?
A: Numerous trails start from the charming municipalities surrounding the park, such as Ronda, Tolox, Yunquera, and El Burgo. These towns often provide convenient access to trailheads and local amenities.

Q: Is it possible to hike with dogs in Sierra de las Nieves National Park?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in Spanish National Parks, but often with restrictions such as being kept on a leash. It's recommended to verify specific regulations with the park authority or local visitor centers, as rules can vary by season or specific protected areas within the park.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sierra de las Nieves?
A: The park offers hiking opportunities year-round, but the best time largely depends on your preferred experience. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially at higher altitudes. Winter offers a unique experience, with snow often covering the higher peaks, though some routes may require specialized gear.
tips: The path enters the Cañada de las Ánimas and crosses a forest of century-old Spanish firs with unique bulging and twisted trunks and branches with leaves in a helical arrangement. The Sierra de las Nieves National Park is the only place on the planet where pinsapos live, a kind of mountain fir that has survived the last ice ages and is an authentic botanical and forest relic.
